titleStmt.title: SII 36.283: original edition by P Shanmugam
No. 283. (A.R. No. 283 of 1910.) TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.

summary.p: Gift of 24 kalams of paddy by Arayan Ādināyakan alias Tiruvaḻundūrnāṭṭu Mūvēndavēḷān, a native of Vāmanamaṅgai in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Rājanārāyaṇa-vaḷanāḍu of Chōḻamaṇḍalam, for feeding 12 itinerant (apūrvi) brāhmaṇas in the temple of Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa of Paḍuvūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Āmūr-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam during the first Amāvāsi in the month of Kārttikai. It was agreed that the annual interest of 12 kalams of paddy shall be utilized for feeding service in the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka cajayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara Urimaiyiṟ
ciṟan=ta maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīṉavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara Ēṉai mannavar Iriyaluṭ cuḻi tarat tikkaṉai
ttun=tan cakkaranaṭātti vīrasimhāsaṉattu Avaṉimuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum vīṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovirāja
kesaripanmarāṉa cakravarttikaḷ śrī kulottuṅkacoḻadevarkku yāṇṭu 4 10 3 Āvatu coḻa maṇṭalattu
rājanārāyaṇa vaḷanāṭṭut tiruvaḻuntūr nāṭṭu vāmanamaṅkai vāmanamaṅkai Uṭaiyān Arayan Ātināyakaṉāna tiruva
ḻuntūr nāṭṭu mūven=taveḷān jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu Āmūr koṭṭattup paṭuvūr nāṭṭu devatāna tiruviṭa
vantai Āḻvār kārttikai māsa mutal Amāvāsitoṟum Apūrvikaḷ panṉiruvar brāhmaṇaṟku bhojaṉamaṭṭuvi
kkakaṭavatāka tevar paṇṭārattile mutalākap poliyūṭukkuṭutta nellu Irupattu nāṟ kalam Irupat
tunāṟ kalattukkum kalattiṉvāyt tūṇip patakkup polivatāṉa nellup panṉiru kalamuṅ
kaikkoṇṭu brāhmaṇanoruvanukku nelluk kuṟuṇiyāp paṉniruvaṟkum I
ṭakkaṭavarkaḷākat tevakanmiyu śrīvayiṣṇavāriyanum śrīkāriyañ ceyvāṉum viṭuvittu kaṇkāṇip
paṭiyeyūṭṭak kaṭavarkaḷāka kaikkoṇṭārkaḷ Ittaṉmam śrī vayiṣṇava rakṣai
Digital edition of SII 36.283 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
